title: Position the gripper and lock out the tipper before gripper replacement removal
summary: Prepare the selected gripper for removal by stopping the cycle, entering jog control, positioning the tipper, supporting the carriages if moved from home, locking out the tipper at the station e-panel, and confirming the correct left or right gripper selection.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when preparing for gripper replacement removal and the gripper must be positioned and the tipper locked out before further maintenance work.
not_for: Do not use this as the complete gripper replacement procedure; the supplied source excerpt only covers preparation and removal setup steps.
Do not use this procedure to perform the remaining physical gripper removal steps, which are not provided in this packet.

responsibilities: Navigate the operator station HMI to the required screen.
Stop the cycle and place the tipper in jog mode.
Move the selected tipper axis to the desired gripper position.
Install carriage support screws if the gripper is moved from home position.
Lock out the tipper at the station e-panel.
Verify the correct left or right gripper is selected before continuing.

safety_notes: Use caution when lifting because components may be heavy.
Failure to follow the caution can result in injury or equipment damage.
Lock out the tipper at the station e-panel before continuing beyond positioning and setup.

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
Access to the station e-panel for lockout/tagout
system_preconditions: The tipper is available to be stopped from cycle operation and manually jogged.
The technician knows which gripper, left or right, is intended for replacement.

external_references.details: Referenced by the source as page 2 for the lockout/tagout instruction.

visual_references.description: Operator station HMI screen used to stop the cycle, enter jog mode, and manually move tipper axes.
Maintenance figure referenced by the candidate as image support for positioning/support context near A-axis removal and carriage support.

steps.title: Open the tipper control screen at the operator station
steps.purpose: Access the HMI screen used for stopping the cycle and jogging the tipper.
steps.instruction: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen.
steps.expected_result: The operator station displays the tipper control screen needed for manual control.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI is on the tipper control screen and the controls needed for cycle stop and jog are available.
steps.failure_condition: The required HMI screen cannot be reached or the expected controls are not available.

actions.label: Navigate to tipper control screen
actions.exact_action: Navigate to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen at the operator station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The operator station screen used for manual jogging of the tipper axes; use it as visual support for the cycle stop and jog workflow.
General maintenance positioning context referenced by the candidate for A-axis removal and re-assembly support.

stop_or_escalate_if: The HMI screen cannot be accessed.
The expected tipper control interface is not present.

source_evidence.text: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen. [Image support needed: Maintenance figure for the gearbox referenced in the A-axis removal and re-assembly procedure.]
Page 83 ... states that operators should navigate to the Visu_Dual_MCP screen using F3, press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle, and then press JOG to open the jogging screen.

steps.title: Stop the cycle
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a stopped state before manual positioning.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the cycle.
steps.healthy_condition: The cycle is stopped and the tipper is no longer running automatically.
steps.failure_condition: The cycle does not stop or remains in automatic operation.

actions.label: Stop cycle
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the cycle.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The HMI area used to stop the automatic tipping cycle before jogging.

stop_or_escalate_if: CYCLE STOP does not stop the cycle.
The tipper remains in automatic operation.

source_evidence.text: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the cycle.
Nearby text states that operators should ... press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.

steps.title: Enter jog mode
steps.purpose: Enable manual control of the tipper for positioning the gripper.
steps.instruction: Press JOG to manually control the tipper.
steps.expected_result: The jogging screen opens and manual axis controls are available.
steps.healthy_condition: Jog mode is active and the axis controls can be used.
steps.failure_condition: Jog mode does not open or manual axis controls are unavailable.

actions.label: Open jog control
actions.exact_action: Press JOG to manually control the tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The jogging screen with axis controls for left and right tipper movement.

stop_or_escalate_if: JOG does not open the jogging screen.
Axis controls are not available after entering jog mode.

source_evidence.text: Press JOG to manually control the tipper.

Nearby text states ... press JOG to open the jogging screen. The manual mode interface includes Z1 AXIS and A1 AXIS controls for the left tipper, Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS controls for the right tipper, and plus/minus buttons to move the selected axis.

steps.title: Move the selected tipper to the desired gripper position
steps.purpose: Position the gripper for removal access.
steps.instruction: Use the axis buttons for the corresponding tipper to move the gripper to the desired location.
steps.expected_result: The selected gripper is moved to the desired removal position.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct tipper axis responds and the gripper reaches the intended location.
steps.failure_condition: The wrong axis is selected, the gripper does not move as intended, or the desired position cannot be reached.

actions.label: Jog tipper axis
actions.exact_action: Use the axis buttons for the corresponding tipper to move the gripper to the desired location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Z1/A1 controls for the left tipper and Z2/A2 controls for the right tipper, including plus/minus movement buttons.

stop_or_escalate_if: The selected axis does not respond.
The wrong tipper or axis appears to be selected.
The gripper cannot be positioned as needed for removal.

source_evidence.text: Use the axis buttons for the corresponding tipper to move the gripper to the desired location.
The manual mode interface includes Z1 AXIS and A1 AXIS controls for the left tipper, Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS controls for the right tipper, and plus/minus buttons to move the selected axis.

steps.title: Support the carriages if moved from home position
steps.purpose: Prevent carriage drop or unsupported condition after moving the gripper from home.
steps.instruction: If the gripper is moved from its home position, thread screws into the threaded holes in the column to support the carriages between the pair of holes.
steps.expected_result: Support screws are installed when required and the carriages are supported.
steps.healthy_condition: The carriages are supported between the pair of holes when the gripper has been moved from home position.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper has been moved from home position and the carriages are not supported.

actions.label: Install carriage support screws
actions.exact_action: Thread screws into the threaded holes in the column to support the carriages between the pair of holes if the gripper is moved from its home position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Support and positioning context for carriage retention during A-axis-related maintenance.
Example maintenance context showing positioning between threaded holes and use of support screws to prevent dropping.
Additional maintenance context for positioning between threaded holes and installing M6 support screws.

stop_or_escalate_if: The gripper has been moved from home position and support cannot be installed.
There is any sign the carriages may drop or are not properly supported.

source_evidence.text: If the gripper is moved from its home position, thread screws into the threaded holes in the column to support the carriages between the pair of holes.
Removal steps near this figure include ... inserting M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.

The removal steps instruct the technician to manually position the A-axis and counterbalance between threaded holes in the actuator base ... and install M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping when belt tension is released.

The removal steps instruct technicians to ... position the A-axis and counterbalance between threaded holes in the actuator base before inserting M6 socket-head screws to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.

steps.title: Lock out the tipper at the station e-panel
steps.purpose: Place the tipper in a safe locked-out state before continuing with removal work.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper at the station e-panel as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out at the station e-panel.
steps.healthy_condition: Lockout/tagout has been applied at the station e-panel in accordance with the referenced lockout/tagout instruction.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out or lockout/tagout cannot be completed.

actions.label: Apply lockout/tagout
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper at the station e-panel as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: General maintenance figure referenced by the candidate as image support for this preparation stage.

stop_or_escalate_if: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed at the station e-panel.
There is any uncertainty that the tipper is fully locked out before continuing.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper at the station e-panel as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2. [Image support needed: Maintenance figure for the gearbox referenced in the A-axis removal and re-assembly procedure.]

steps.title: Verify the correct gripper selection
steps.purpose: Confirm that the intended left or right gripper is the one prepared for removal.
steps.instruction: Verify the correct gripper has been selected, either the left gripper or the right gripper.
steps.expected_result: The intended gripper selection is confirmed before removal continues.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct left or right gripper is positively identified and matches the planned maintenance task.
steps.failure_condition: The selected gripper cannot be confirmed or appears to be the wrong side.

actions.label: Confirm gripper side
actions.exact_action: Verify the correct gripper has been selected, either the left gripper or the right gripper.

stop_or_escalate_if: The left or right gripper selection cannot be confirmed.
There is any doubt that the correct gripper is selected for removal.

source_evidence.text: Verify the correct gripper has been selected, either the left gripper or the right gripper.

success_criteria: The gripper is positioned as needed for removal.
Carriage support is installed if the gripper was moved from home position.
The tipper is locked out at the station e-panel.
The correct left or right gripper selection is confirmed.
healthy_conditions: The operator station HMI allows access to the tipper control and jog functions.
The selected tipper axis can be manually moved to the desired location.
Carriages are supported when the gripper is moved from home position.
Lockout/tagout is applied at the station e-panel before further removal work.
The intended gripper side is clearly identified.
failure_conditions: The required HMI screen cannot be accessed.
Jog mode or axis controls are unavailable.
The gripper cannot be positioned as needed.
Carriage support cannot be installed when required.
The tipper cannot be locked out at the station e-panel.
The correct gripper selection cannot be confirmed.
The source excerpt does not provide the remaining physical removal steps.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the HMI does not provide the expected cycle stop or jog controls.
Stop and escalate if the gripper cannot be positioned safely or the correct axis cannot be controlled.
Stop and escalate if carriage support cannot be installed after moving from home position.
Do not continue with removal until lockout/tagout is completed at the station e-panel.
Escalate for SME review because the supplied source excerpt appears truncated and does not include the full gripper replacement procedure.

Maintenance figure referenced by the candidate as image support for preparation and support context.
